mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual tightness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines for 3+ consecutive months but SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of such periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1200 thousand units annualized and retail sales (RSAFS) show month-over-month contraction, aggregate demand weakens, signaling recession risk that drives negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking markets reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 6-month trend, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth accelerates but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of such periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts, signaling a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: Both UNRATE and PAYEMS show weakness but SPX 60-day forward returns exceed +5% in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 4.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the cost of capital for equity valuations and stimulates borrowing-dependent demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS falls below 4.0% but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) contract, indicating a demand-side slowdown that compresses earnings multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS 3-month growth < 0%
failure_condition: Both GDP and retail sales show weakness but SPX 60-day forward returns exceed +3% in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises, signaling simultaneous supply-side weakness and labor market softening that reduces earnings capacity and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% for 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
failure_condition: Both INDPRO and UNRATE show weakness but SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
